# AIDONE

> A product-facing acceptance protocol for AI-generated code. AIDONE helps founders, product people, designers, creators, and solo builders verify that AI-coded software is safe, maintainable, testable, and ready to hand to users.

## Core Docs

- [README](./README.md): Project overview, positioning, and quick start.
- [AIDONE](./AIDONE.md): Core acceptance protocol.

## Examples

- [AGENTS Example](./examples/AGENTS.md): Minimal AGENTS.md snippet for projects using AIDONE.
- [CLAUDE Example](./examples/CLAUDE.md): Minimal Claude Code project instruction.
- [Review Prompt](./examples/review-prompt.md): Prompt for reviewing a project with AIDONE.
- [Delivery Template](./examples/delivery-template.md): Final response template for AI coding agents.

## Checklists

- [Security](./checklists/security.md): Additional security acceptance checks.
- [i18n](./checklists/i18n.md): User-facing text and localization acceptance checks.
- [Accessibility](./checklists/accessibility.md): Basic accessibility acceptance checks.
- [Testing](./checklists/testing.md): Behaviour-focused testing acceptance checks.

## References

- [Sources](./references/sources.md): Source map and related AI-era project file standards.
